personal.name: Amjad Rehawi
personal.tagline: Hardware engineer for things that move themselves.
personal.subTagline: Senior honors EE + ME at Santa Clara University. Pivoting from EV / power electronics into drones and aerospace — with the Raven eVTOL swarm as the proof.
personal.degree: B.S. Electrical Engineering & B.S. Mechanical Engineering (Honors)

brandThesis.headline: The same skill stack that ships a 420 V electric race car is what makes an autonomous aircraft fly itself.
brandThesis.body: Power electronics, BMS, CAN bus, STM32 firmware, PCB safety review. That's the stack I've been building on Bronco Racing's >400 V Formula SAE car and on motor-drive validation at STMicroelectronics — and it's the stack the next generation of unmanned aircraft will run on. Raven is the pivot in flight: an 8-stage roadmap from a tilt-rotor eVTOL to a 4-drone autonomous swarm with onboard AI, sensor-fusion collision avoidance, and wireless autonomous charging at home base.

about.heading: About
paragraphs: Senior honors student at Santa Clara University, double major in Electrical and Mechanical Engineering. 3.8 GPA. Faculty-nominated for the Possible Career Accelerator (1 of 25). SolidWorks CSWP and MakerLab certified. A-Levels from the University of Cambridge in Computer Science (A*, 92%) and Physics (A).

Over the past two years I've worked across high-voltage motorsport electronics on Bronco Racing's Formula SAE EV (420 V / 540 V BMS, CAN bus, charging, PCB safety review), and across motor-drive validation at STMicroelectronics — debugging a custom traction-motor inverter on Lauterbach JTAG, authoring parameterization guides on PWM and space-vector modulation, building an end-to-end STM32 proximity-sensing system from ADC to speaker amp.

Raven is where it all converges. A tilt-rotor eVTOL I'm building as my senior design project, on a self-directed 8-stage roadmap toward a 4-drone autonomous swarm. The thesis is simple: motor-inverter debug is ESC debug. SVM is the same math that drives a brushless drone motor. JTAG-traced firmware on a 540 V pack is the same discipline that keeps an autonomous aircraft from falling out of the sky.

projects.name: Raven
projects.subtitle: Tilt-rotor eVTOL — the first airframe in a 4-drone autonomous swarm with onboard AI and wireless autonomous charging.
projects.summary: Raven is a tilt-rotor eVTOL — fixed-wing in cruise, three-motor VTOL in hover — built as the first airframe in a planned 4-drone autonomous swarm with onboard AI, sensor-fusion collision avoidance, and wireless autonomous charging at home base. Custom redesign on top of the Flightory Stallion FPV airframe: re-spec'd to PA612-CF15 carbon-fiber composite, custom carbon-fiber tubes, self-selected avionics, and a modular multi-segment fuselage. MECH115 Advanced CAD group project (Spring 2026); designated senior design project.

hero.pitch: Fixed-wing in cruise, three-motor VTOL in hover — the first airframe in a 4-drone autonomous swarm.
highlights: Tilt-rotor configuration: 2 wing-mounted motors active in fixed-wing, all 3 motors active in VTOL
Eppler E205 airfoil, CFD-optimized for 60–70 km/h cruise
Primary structure in PA612-CF15 carbon-fiber composite, printed on a Bambu P2S Combo / X2D
Modular fuselage (FUS 1–5 L/R), detachable wings, interchangeable nose for payload swaps
Snap-fit wing locks with torsion springs, AC hinges, M3 inserts, V-tail with rudders
Self-selected programmable servos for ailerons, flaps, rudders, tail
FAA Part 107 paperwork done early in Stage 1

sections.heading: What "I designed the parts" means
sections.body: Starting from stock Stallion STLs but changing material, constraints, specs and CAD geometry to match new design objectives. Every part is reverse-engineered, re-validated, re-optimized.
items: CAD: re-spec to PA612-CF15 load constraints; modify geometry where stock parts don't survive Raven's flight envelope
FEA: static loading on the carbon-fiber main spar (bending), motor mounts (thrust + vibration), tail boom mount (aerodynamic loads)
CFD: airfoil performance plus full-airframe flow at cruise
Materials characterization: tensile and torsion testing on PA612-CF15 printed coupons; FDM/FFF print-setting tuning
Kinematics: aileron/rudder servo-to-pushrod linkages, snap-fit lock with torsion springs
Electronics & avionics: flight controller, ESCs, motors, receiver, GPS selection for Stage 1; FAA Part 107-compliant wiring

sections.heading: Why this is the pivot in flight
sections.body: Motor-inverter debug at STMicroelectronics is ESC debug here. Space-vector modulation is the math that drives every brushless motor on this airframe. JTAG-traced firmware on a 540 V FSAE pack is the same discipline that keeps an autonomous aircraft from falling out of the sky. Raven is the proof that the EV power-electronics stack ports cleanly into aerospace.

sections.heading: Stage 1 work (May 2026)
sections.body: Printing parts in PA612-CF15; tensile / torsion testing on coupons; FDM/FFF print-setting optimization; FEA on main spar, motor mounts, and tail boom mount; CFD pass on the full airframe at cruise; servo selection and programming; avionics integration; RC test flight prep; FAA Part 107 registration; engineering report.

roadmap.title: Mechanical build + RC flight
roadmap.desc: Airframe complete; off-the-shelf flight controller, ESCs, motors, receiver, GPS; manual RC control; FAA Part 107 done early.

roadmap.title: Autonomous flight
roadmap.desc: Programmable flight controller; GPS waypointing; basic mission planning.

roadmap.title: 4-drone swarm
roadmap.desc: Build 3 more Raven airframes; coordinated synchronized flight.

roadmap.title: Application — object detection
roadmap.desc: Swarm flies pre-programmed open-field pattern, detects pre-trained objects, reports GPS coordinates to HQ.

roadmap.title: HQ-side AI
roadmap.desc: AI lives at HQ; drones stream live video; HQ does analysis, decision-making, and mid-mission re-planning.

roadmap.title: Onboard control AI
roadmap.desc: AI moves onboard for adaptive systems-level control in harsh / uncertain conditions (fire, wind, turbulence).

roadmap.title: Onboard sensor-fusion AI
roadmap.desc: Fixed-wing ↔ VTOL transition, onboard object detection, swarm-internal + foreign-object collision avoidance.

roadmap.title: Full IoT + wireless autonomous charging
roadmap.desc: HQ AI dispatches coverage areas; drones return-to-base, land in wireless charging stations, recharge. Zero human in the loop.

projects.name: Bronco Racing FSAE
projects.subtitle: HV & co-electrical lead on Santa Clara's >400 V Formula SAE EV. 420 V / 540 V BMS, CAN bus, programmable charging, PCB safety review.
projects.summary: End-to-end ownership of the tractive system electrical stack on Bronco Racing's Formula SAE Electric car. BMS programming, programmable external charging system, CAN bus architecture, STM32 firmware, PCB safety review, and 140+ thermistors physically integrated into the accumulator harness.

hero.pitch: End-to-end electrical ownership on a >400 V FSAE EV — BMS, CAN, charging, PCB review, thermal harness.
highlights: Programmed a 420 V / 540 V BMS — cell-voltage monitoring, temperature sensing, SoC algorithms, safety interlocks
Configured Orion BMS 2 for the accumulator charging system; designed thermal-management expansion modules
Designed and built a programmable external charging system with current/voltage control and thermal-based safety shutoff; verified FSAE EV rules compliance
Designed schematic + PCB sections for the shutdown and charging shutdown circuits; caught protection-logic errors before fab
Reviewed precharge, discharge, and IMD (insulation monitoring) PCB designs for FSAE EV compliance
Architected the vehicle CAN bus network; wrote STM32 firmware for real-time sensor interfacing and diagnostics
Built a Python GUI for real-time CAN message monitoring and automated thermal-management diagnostics
Soldered and integrated 140+ thermistors into the accumulator harness; placement optimization improved measurement accuracy by ~50%

sections.heading: What I own
items: Programmable external charging system + safety protocols
Shutdown circuit and charging shutdown circuit schematics + PCB sections
Vehicle CAN bus network architecture and STM32 firmware
Python GUI for CAN diagnostics on the thermal subsystem
Thermistor placement strategy and accumulator wiring harness
Tractive system layout — HV routing and subsystem placement

sections.heading: Leadership
sections.body: I lead team training on precision soldering, thermal system architecture, and HV safety protocols. I coordinate cross-functional handoffs between BMS, CAN, power electronics, and thermal management.

projects.name: STMicroelectronics
projects.subtitle: Application Engineer Intern — debugged a custom traction motor inverter on Lauterbach JTAG; authored SVM/PWM guides for ST customers.
projects.summary: Supported Global Key Account Customers (GKAC) in microcontroller project development across automotive and industrial applications. Bridged ST silicon and customer products. Motor-inverter debug, embedded firmware bring-up, parameterization guides, and competitive teardowns.

hero.pitch: Debugged a custom traction motor inverter on Lauterbach JTAG — and translated the math to ST customers.
highlights: Debugged a custom traction motor inverter using Lauterbach JTAG hardware and StellarStudio IDE — resolved PWM timing faults, gate-driver issues, and current-sensing calibration errors
Diagnosed independently that the DC bus required stabilization; proposed and implemented a parallel-capacitor fix between inverter and supply
Built an STM32-based proximity sensing system end to end: ADC signal processing → timed interrupt logic → PWM/DAC audio output → analog low-pass filter → amplifier → speaker
Identified firmware bugs in motor control algorithms and provided corrected code
Authored parameterization guides covering PWM and space-vector modulation (SVM) theory for internal and customer-facing use
Conducted hardware teardowns and reverse-engineering analysis of competitive automotive electronics modules; delivered Excel pivot-table analyses and PowerPoint presentations to ST marketing teams

sections.heading: Why this transfers to drones
sections.body: Motor-inverter debug ↔ drone ESC debug. SVM / PWM is the same math that drives brushless drone motors. JTAG / Lauterbach is the same toolset used for flight-controller firmware. The pivot from EV to drone is a continuation, not a switch.

projects.name: PalmSync
projects.subtitle: Biometric wellness wearable — stress monitoring + smart ambient lighting. 1st Place, Asearis Hackathon. Patent pending.
projects.summary: Dual-purpose biometric wellness device combining real-time stress monitoring with smart ambient lighting. PPG (HR/HRV) + EDA (electrodermal activity) + thermal sensors on an ESP32 for wireless, with an AI-powered analytics platform turning raw biosignals into actionable wellness insights. Concept to working prototype in under 6 weeks.

hero.pitch: Stress monitoring + smart ambient lighting in one device. Concept to working prototype in under 6 weeks.
highlights: Team Captain & Product Lead, 3-engineer team
Concept to working prototype in under 6 weeks
Sensors: PPG (HR/HRV), EDA (electrodermal activity), thermal
Compute / connectivity: ESP32 (wireless)
Analytics: AI-powered platform turning raw biosignals into actionable wellness insights
BOM tradeoffs against a $14.82 target — cost, wearability, manufacturability
Positioned for a $49.99 retail price point in a $50B+ wearable-tech market
1st Place, Asearis Hackathon · Patent currently being pursued

projects.name: Build-a-Bronco
projects.subtitle: ENGR 172 Applied Entrepreneurship — modular multi-color 3D-printed Bronco mascot. SCU Athletics client, bookstore sales imminent.
projects.summary: Modular, multi-color 3D-printed Bronco mascot where parts cross-fit assemble like Lego. Client: SCU Athletics (~10,000 customer base). Sales channel: SCU Campus Bookstore, launching about three weeks from May 2026. Instructor: Dr. Christopher Kitts.

hero.pitch: Modular Lego-style Bronco mascot, going on sale at the SCU Bookstore.
highlights: Designed and ran the Build-a-Bronco Product Design Survey; collected customer-needs data
AI-aided data analysis to turn raw survey responses into graphical interpretations
Researched AI applications in manufacturing, detailed design, and product packaging
Designed the Bronco from scratch in CAD; designed the cross-fit snap-together assembly
Optimized for 3D printability — orientation, supports, tolerances
Optimized print settings, materials, and multi-color printing strategy in the SCU Maker Lab

projects.name: CrossFit Maker Challenge — Megatrons
projects.subtitle: Sponsored by CrossFit LLC, tied to the CrossFit Games' 20th anniversary commemorative product. Past Phase A, in Phase B (manufacturing).
projects.summary: CrossFit LLC-sponsored commemorative desk sculpture tied to the CrossFit Games' 20th anniversary. Visited every CrossFit gym in the South Bay; designed quantitative and qualitative research instruments; passed Phase A on the April 17 pitch.

hero.pitch: Sponsored commemorative product design, grounded in South Bay field research.
highlights: Visited every CrossFit gym in the South Bay; interviewed gymgoers
Designed a quantitative Commemorative Desk Sculpture Survey + structured interviewer guide
Synthesized gymgoer preferences vs. CrossFit-as-sponsor priorities
Multiple concept sketches and design iterations; Gemini AI image generation for early ideation
SVG / CAD design iterations
Built and delivered the April 17 pitch that passed Phase A
